title: Flow cytometry assay and cell staining protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bo3fg","text":"This protocol describes antigen stimulation of human peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC), staining for various surface and intracellular molecular markers, followed by flow cytometry measurements. PBMC contain various T and NK cell populations, which produce cytokines upon specific stimulation with antigens. T (and NK) cells can be stimulated in various ways: using whole virus, bacteria (or membrane fractions of bacterial lysates), antigenic peptides etc. The stimulation guide provided with this protocol refers to stimulation with antigenic peptides, influenza virus and a standard cell mitogen (Concanavalin A, ConA), though it can be adapted to incorporate any other cell stimulant.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ofhe","text":"This method is used to stimulate influenza specific cytokine production. After PBMC stimulation, cells are stained to detect cytokine producing T (and/or NK) cells by flow cytometry. Antigens are presented by antigen presenting cells (APC), which are also present among PBMC. In this procedure the Golgi apparatus is blocked by Brefeldin A and Monensin in order to keep all the cytokines produced after stimulation within the cell. The number of influenza specific cytokine producing T (and/or NK) cells can be determined by staining cells with fluorophore conjugated antibodies specific for the cytokine(s) of interest and counted on a flow cytometer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Miloje Savic
